headline: Macquarie Downgrades PayPal as Cost-Saving Plan Looks Too Back-End Loaded
summary: PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) stock picked up a fresh analyst downgrade this morning, with Macquarie analyst Paul Golding cutting PYPL to Neutral from Outperform and lowering his price target to $50 from $58, citing a soft Q2 2026 outlook and a cost-saving plan that’s heavily back-end loaded. The takeaway for prudent investors: even with a Q1 2026 ... Macquarie Downgrades PayPal as Cost-Saving Plan Looks Too Back-End Loaded

headline: Texas Super Kings Announce Xoom as Official Remittance Sponsor for 2026 Season
summary: The Texas Super Kings (TSK) are proud to announce a collaboration with Xoom, a PayPal service and leading digital remittance provider. In a first-of-its-kind association for the franchise, Xoom joins as the Official Remittance Sponsor, the first remittance brand to partner with Texas Super Kings and will also serve as the Leading Headgear Sponsor for the 2026 season.

headline: Is It Time To Reassess PayPal (PYPL) After A 34% One Year Share Price Slide?
summary: This article examines whether PayPal Holdings, at around US$46 a share, still aligns with what it might be worth by breaking down what the current price could be implying about the stock. The stock has seen a 7.7% decline over the last week, a 3.1% gain over 30 days, and is currently showing a 20.4% decline year to date and a 34.0% decline over the last year. Recent coverage has focused on how investors are reassessing large, established payment platforms as competitive pressures and...
